free_text: The incident 3.40 MeV neutrons were produced
by the 7Li(p,n0)7Be reaction using a thin target.
The target consisted of 99.8% enriched 7Li evaporated
.The separated isotope tungsten samples were in the
form of metallic powders. Each of them was placed in a
polyethylene container 2 cm diam x 5 cm high. The four
samples had the same number of atoms (0.215 mole).
The dimensions, masses, and enrichments are presented
The scattered neutrons were detected by an
array of four recoil proton detectors placed at 20 deg
intervals. Each detector consisted of a
10 cm diam. x 2.5 cm thick NE-213 liquid scintillator
optically coupled to an XP-1040 photomultiplier tube.
Each detector was housed in a massive shield of
paraffin loaded with lithium carbonate and borax.
The primary neutron flux was monitored by
an auxiliary neutron detector In the time-of-flight
mode, and also by counting with a Ge(Li) diode the
431- and 478-keV gamma rays produced by proton
* Counting statistics in the peak and background
Error of the dispersion of monitor
Data received from Dr. Pascal Romain
